story.title: AI Found a Root Bug in Linux That Everyone Missed for 15 Years
story.summary: Plus: The Pentagon is training amateurs to become part of its hacker army, a Flock license plate reader error led to cops surrounding a car reviewer, and more.

points.heading: Critical Linux Bug Found
points.detail: GhostLock, a use-after-free bug, sat in the Linux kernel for 15 years, allowing any logged-in user to take root.

points.heading: Widespread Vulnerability
points.detail: The flaw shipped by default in essentially every mainstream distribution since 2011, requiring no special permissions.

points.heading: AI-Driven Discovery
points.detail: Nebula found the bug with VEGA, its AI-driven bug-hunting tool, part of a run of Linux privilege-escalation flaws.

points.heading: Exploit Reliable, Patches Uneven
points.detail: Nebula’s exploit was 97 percent reliable; the bug was fixed in April, but patch availability is uneven.
